codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly adds binary file detection to ReadFileTool with allow_binary opt-in flag for base64-encoded reads. Binary write rejection is handled via file_guardrails module. All 6 expected behaviors are covered by tests.

sharedStateObservations.pattern: ReadFileTool::new().with_allow_binary(true) builder pattern exists but execute() always uses args.allow_binary from JSON input, making the builder method effectively unused at runtime
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tools.rs:27 - allow_binary field exists, with_allow_binary() setter exists (line 46-50), but execute() uses args.allow_binary (line 180) which defaults to false from serde
sharedStateObservations.suggestedAction: Either remove the unused builder method or document that it only affects the JSON schema default value, not runtime behavior

summary: Feature implementation passes review. Binary detection is correctly implemented using detect_binary_content() which checks first 8192 bytes for null bytes and magic bytes (ELF, PNG, JPEG, PDF, ZIP + 30+ additional formats). Binary files are rejected by default with clear error messages, and allowed with allow_binary=true returning base64-encoded content. Tests verify all expected behaviors. Binary write rejection is properly handled via file_guardrails module.
